Title: Verify primary action button label and placement are consistent across registration, encounter and order forms
Preconditions: Logged-in clinician with access to registration, clinical form and order entry
Test_Steps: 1. Open patient registration form and note the save/submit control label and position | 2. Open a clinical encounter form and note the save control | 3. Open an order entry form and note the save control | 4. Compare label wording, casing and screen position across all three
Expected_Result: Primary save control uses consistent label wording, casing, color and bottom-right (or consistent) placement across all three forms; no synonyms like Save vs Submit vs Confirm for the same action

Title: Verify date format is rendered consistently across dashboard, encounters list and reports
Preconditions: Patient with multiple encounters and at least one report available
Test_Steps: 1. Open patient dashboard and note date format of recent encounters | 2. Open the visits/encounters list and note date format | 3. Open a report containing dates and note format | 4. Compare formats for order, separators and locale
Expected_Result: All surfaces render dates in the same locale-aware format (order, separator, 12/24h); no mixed dd/mm vs mm/dd within the same locale

Title: Verify terminology for the same concept is identical across navigation, page title and breadcrumb
Preconditions: Any navigable module page available
Test_Steps: 1. Navigate to a module via the main menu and note the menu label | 2. Observe the page heading/title on the loaded page | 3. Observe the breadcrumb trail entry for that page | 4. Compare all three labels
Expected_Result: Menu label, page title and breadcrumb use the same canonical term for the module; no drift such as Visits vs Encounters vs Consultations for one concept

Title: Verify confirmation prompt appears before deleting a saved encounter (destructive action)
Preconditions: Clinician with a saved encounter and delete permission
Test_Steps: 1. Open a patient with a saved encounter | 2. Choose to delete/void the encounter | 3. Observe the interstitial confirmation | 4. Cancel and verify encounter persists | 5. Confirm and verify encounter is voided
Expected_Result: A confirmation dialog with clear consequence text and an explicit reason field appears before voiding; Cancel preserves the encounter; Confirm voids it and records who/when/why

Title: Verify destructive confirmation requires a void reason and rejects empty reason
Preconditions: Clinician voiding a clinical record requiring a reason
Test_Steps: 1. Initiate void of an observation or encounter | 2. Leave the reason field blank | 3. Attempt to confirm | 4. Observe validation | 5. Enter a valid reason and confirm
Expected_Result: With blank reason the confirm is blocked and an inline message requests a reason; with a valid reason the void completes and the reason is stored in audit

Title: Verify unsaved-changes guard warns before navigating away from a partially completed form
Preconditions: Clinician with a clinical form partially filled and not saved
Test_Steps: 1. Open a clinical form and enter data in several fields | 2. Without saving, click a navigation link or browser back | 3. Observe the leave-confirmation prompt | 4. Choose Stay and verify data intact | 5. Repeat and choose Leave
Expected_Result: A leave-confirmation warns that unsaved changes will be lost; Stay returns to the form with all entered data intact; Leave discards and navigates away

Title: Verify decision-table behavior for save/cancel/navigate combinations on a dirty form
Preconditions: Clinician on a clinical form
Test_Steps: 1. For each row apply the form state and user action | 2. Observe whether a guard prompt appears and the final state | 3. Verify data retention matches expected
Test_Data: Rules: Clean+Navigate=no prompt,leave; Dirty+Navigate=prompt; Dirty+Save=no prompt,persist; Dirty+Cancel=prompt then discard; Clean+Cancel=close no prompt
Expected_Result: Each combination matches the decision table: prompts appear only when the form is dirty and the action loses data; clean states navigate without interruption; saved data persists

Title: Verify duplicate-submission protection when clinician double-clicks Save on an order
Preconditions: Clinician submitting a drug order
Test_Steps: 1. Fill a drug order form | 2. Rapidly double-click the Save control | 3. Observe the network and resulting orders | 4. Check the orders list for duplicates
Expected_Result: Only one order is created; the control disables or debounces after first click and shows a progress state; no duplicate order appears in the list

Title: Verify form repopulates entered data after a server-side validation error
Preconditions: Clinician submitting a form that triggers server validation failure
Test_Steps: 1. Fill a registration form including several fields | 2. Enter one value that fails server validation | 3. Submit | 4. Observe the returned form
Test_Data: Field with invalid value=Phone 'abc'; other fields valid
Expected_Result: Server rejects submission but all previously entered valid fields remain populated; only the offending field is flagged; no full re-entry required

Title: Verify graceful recovery and retry when a save fails due to transient network error
Preconditions: Clinician saving a clinical form with simulated network interruption
Test_Steps: 1. Fill a clinical form | 2. Simulate a network drop and attempt to save | 3. Observe the error state | 4. Restore network and use the offered retry | 5. Verify single save
Expected_Result: A clear, non-technical error is shown with a retry option; entered data is preserved; retry succeeds and creates exactly one record without duplication

Title: Verify validation messages are specific, actionable and field-anchored
Preconditions: Clinician on registration with multiple required fields
Test_Steps: 1. Submit the registration form with several invalid/empty fields | 2. Observe each validation message | 3. Verify message text names the field and the correction needed | 4. Verify focus moves to the first error
Test_Data: Given name='' ; DOB future date; Phone='12'
Expected_Result: Each message is specific (names field and rule, e.g. 'Date of birth cannot be in the future'), appears adjacent to its field, and focus lands on the first invalid field

Title: Verify system errors avoid raw stack traces and present a user-safe message with reference id
Preconditions: Ability to trigger a server error condition
Test_Steps: 1. Trigger a server-side error (e.g. malformed request) | 2. Observe the displayed error | 3. Inspect for stack traces, SQL or class names | 4. Note any support reference id
Expected_Result: User sees a friendly message with a correlation/reference id; no stack trace, SQL, internal class names or server paths are exposed in the UI

Title: Verify success confirmation is shown after saving an encounter and auto-dismisses appropriately
Preconditions: Clinician saving a valid encounter
Test_Steps: 1. Save a valid clinical encounter | 2. Observe the success toast/banner | 3. Verify wording confirms what was saved | 4. Verify it is dismissible and screen-reader announced
Expected_Result: A success message confirms the specific action (e.g. 'Vitals encounter saved'), is announced to assistive tech via a live region, and can be dismissed or auto-dismisses without blocking work

Title: Verify clinician can reach order entry from patient search within a minimal click path
Preconditions: Clinician logged in at the home/search screen
Test_Steps: 1. From home, search a patient | 2. Open the patient | 3. Navigate to order entry | 4. Count clicks and page loads from search result to order form
Expected_Result: Order entry is reachable in a small, predictable number of steps from the patient result with no dead-ends; each step has a clear affordance and the active patient context is preserved

Title: Verify breadcrumb allows direct return to a prior level without losing patient context
Preconditions: Clinician three levels deep within a patient context
Test_Steps: 1. Navigate patient > dashboard > encounter detail | 2. Click an intermediate breadcrumb level | 3. Verify the prior page loads | 4. Verify the same patient remains active
Expected_Result: Breadcrumb navigates directly to the chosen ancestor level; the active patient context is retained; no full re-search required

Title: Verify active patient context banner persists across module navigation
Preconditions: Clinician with an active patient selected
Test_Steps: 1. Select an active patient | 2. Navigate across dashboard, orders, results and visits | 3. On each page observe the patient identity banner | 4. Verify name, identifier and key demographics remain visible and correct
Expected_Result: The patient identity banner remains pinned and correct across all modules, preventing wrong-patient actions; switching patients clearly updates the banner everywhere

Title: Verify back navigation after deep linking returns to a sensible location not a blank state
Preconditions: User arrives at a deep-linked encounter URL
Test_Steps: 1. Open a deep link directly to an encounter detail | 2. Use in-app back/up navigation | 3. Observe destination | 4. Verify no blank or error page results
Expected_Result: Back/up navigation lands on a meaningful parent (patient dashboard) rather than a blank page or browser error; context is reconstructed correctly

Title: Verify field-level help/tooltips are available for non-obvious clinical inputs
Preconditions: Clinician on a clinical form with coded fields
Test_Steps: 1. Open a clinical form containing a coded or specialized field | 2. Hover/focus the help affordance | 3. Read the help text | 4. Verify it explains expected input and units
Expected_Result: Help text is available via hover and keyboard focus, explains expected input, units and constraints, and is announced to assistive technology

Title: Verify first-time user can complete patient registration using only visible affordances
Preconditions: New clinician account with registration access and no prior training
Test_Steps: 1. As a first-time user, attempt to register a patient using only on-screen cues | 2. Note any step requiring outside knowledge | 3. Record confusion points and dead-ends | 4. Capture time and error count
Expected_Result: User completes registration relying on labels, placeholders and inline help; no step requires undocumented knowledge; confusion points are logged for triage

Title: Verify consistent iconography meaning across modules aids recognition
Preconditions: Multiple modules using shared icons
Test_Steps: 1. Identify icons used for edit, delete/void, print and add across modules | 2. Verify each icon has consistent meaning everywhere | 3. Verify each icon has an accessible name/tooltip
Expected_Result: Each icon carries the same meaning across modules and exposes an accessible name; no icon is reused for conflicting actions

Title: Verify voiding a patient identifier requires explicit double confirmation
Preconditions: Admin with permission to void identifiers
Test_Steps: 1. Open patient identifiers | 2. Choose to void an identifier | 3. Observe primary confirmation | 4. Observe any secondary type-to-confirm or reason requirement | 5. Confirm and verify audit
Expected_Result: Voiding a patient identifier requires explicit confirmation with a reason; the action is audit-logged with actor, timestamp and reason; cancel aborts with no change

Title: Verify retiring a concept/metadata shows impact warning before proceeding
Preconditions: Admin retiring a concept used by existing forms
Test_Steps: 1. Open a concept referenced by active forms | 2. Choose retire | 3. Observe whether dependencies/impact are communicated | 4. Confirm and verify retire state
Expected_Result: Before retiring, the UI warns the metadata is in use and confirmation is required; after retiring it is marked retired (not hard-deleted) preserving historical data integrity

Title: Verify discard-draft confirmation when closing a new order without saving
Preconditions: Clinician with a new unsaved order draft
Test_Steps: 1. Begin a new order and enter details | 2. Click close/cancel without saving | 3. Observe discard confirmation | 4. Choose Keep editing then Discard
Expected_Result: Closing an unsaved order prompts a discard confirmation; Keep editing returns to the draft intact; Discard closes without creating an order

Title: Verify a voided observation can be unvoided restoring original values and audit chain
Preconditions: Admin/clinician with unvoid permission and a voided observation
Test_Steps: 1. Void an observation with a reason | 2. Locate the voided record | 3. Perform unvoid | 4. Verify the observation is restored with original values | 5. Verify audit shows void then unvoid
Expected_Result: Unvoid restores the original observation values; the audit trail retains both void and unvoid events with actor/timestamp; no data is silently lost

Title: Verify undo window/affordance after a non-destructive bulk action where supported
Preconditions: User performs a reversible action that offers undo
Test_Steps: 1. Perform an action that surfaces an undo affordance (e.g. dismiss a notification or remove a list item) | 2. Invoke undo within the offered window | 3. Verify prior state is restored
Test_Data: Action=remove item from a working list
Expected_Result: Undo restores the prior state within the offered window; after the window closes the action is final and any persistence is consistent; clear messaging on undo availability

Title: Verify keyboard-only completion of a clinical form without a mouse
Preconditions: Clinician on a clinical form; keyboard only
Test_Steps: 1. Using Tab/Shift+Tab navigate all fields in logical order | 2. Enter values using keyboard only | 3. Operate dropdowns and date pickers via keyboard | 4. Submit via keyboard | 5. Verify save
Expected_Result: All fields are reachable and operable by keyboard in a logical order; dropdowns/date pickers are keyboard-operable; form submits successfully without a mouse

Title: Verify visible focus indicator is present on all interactive controls
Preconditions: Any page with interactive controls
Test_Steps: 1. Tab through controls on a representative form and dashboard | 2. Observe focus indicator on each control | 3. Verify contrast of the focus ring | 4. Confirm no focus is lost or hidden
Expected_Result: Every interactive control shows a clearly visible, sufficient-contrast focus indicator; focus is never invisible or trapped; tab order matches visual order

Title: Verify modal dialogs trap focus and restore focus to trigger on close
Preconditions: A modal (e.g. confirmation dialog) is invokable
Test_Steps: 1. Open a modal via keyboard | 2. Tab through and verify focus stays within the modal | 3. Press Escape to close | 4. Verify focus returns to the invoking control
Expected_Result: Focus is trapped within the open modal; Escape closes it; on close focus returns to the control that opened it; background is inert

Title: Verify patient with no encounters shows a helpful empty state with a primary action
Preconditions: Newly registered patient with no encounters
Test_Steps: 1. Open a patient with zero encounters | 2. Observe the encounters/visits area | 3. Verify empty-state messaging | 4. Verify a clear next-step action is offered
Expected_Result: An informative empty state explains there are no encounters yet and offers a primary action (e.g. Start a visit) rather than a blank or broken region

Title: Verify patient search with no matches shows guidance rather than a blank grid
Preconditions: Search available; query that matches nothing
Test_Steps: 1. Search a patient with a query guaranteed to return no results | 2. Observe the result area | 3. Verify no-results messaging and suggestions
Expected_Result: A no-results message is shown with guidance (broaden search, check spelling, register new) instead of a blank or error grid

Title: Verify report with no data in range communicates empty range, not failure
Preconditions: Report module with a date range producing no rows
Test_Steps: 1. Run a report for a range known to have no data | 2. Observe the output | 3. Verify it distinguishes empty-result from error
Expected_Result: The report clearly states no data for the selected criteria and remains distinct from an error state; filters remain adjustable without reload errors

Title: Verify long-running search shows a loading indicator and disables duplicate submission
Preconditions: Search likely to take noticeable time
Test_Steps: 1. Initiate a search expected to take time | 2. Observe loading indicator | 3. Attempt to resubmit during load | 4. Verify result rendering
Expected_Result: A loading indicator is shown during the query; resubmission is prevented or queued sensibly; results render without flicker once loaded

Title: Verify skeleton/placeholder prevents layout shift while dashboard widgets load
Preconditions: Patient dashboard with multiple async widgets
Test_Steps: 1. Open a patient dashboard | 2. Observe widgets loading | 3. Watch for content jumping/layout shift | 4. Verify final layout stability
Test_Data: Dashboard with vitals, allergies, problems widgets
Expected_Result: Loading placeholders reserve space so content does not jump; cumulative layout shift is minimal; each widget resolves to data or its own empty/error state

Title: Verify slow API response surfaces timeout messaging instead of indefinite spinner
Preconditions: Ability to simulate a slow/timed-out backend call
Test_Steps: 1. Trigger a data load with simulated backend delay beyond timeout | 2. Observe behavior past the timeout | 3. Verify messaging and retry | 4. Verify spinner does not hang forever
Expected_Result: After the timeout a clear message with retry is shown; the spinner does not persist indefinitely; no partial/corrupt data is displayed as complete

Title: Verify error region is screen-reader announced and programmatically associated with fields
Preconditions: Form with validation; screen reader active
Test_Steps: 1. Submit a form with errors using a screen reader | 2. Verify errors are announced | 3. Verify aria-describedby/aria-invalid links message to field | 4. Navigate to first error
Expected_Result: Validation errors are announced via a live region, fields expose aria-invalid, and messages are programmatically associated so assistive tech users can locate and fix them

Title: Verify partial failure in a multi-section save communicates which section failed
Preconditions: Form with multiple sections where one section can fail server-side
Test_Steps: 1. Submit a multi-section form where one section fails validation | 2. Observe the error presentation | 3. Verify the failing section is identified | 4. Verify successful sections state
Expected_Result: The UI clearly identifies which section failed and why, preserves valid sections, and avoids implying the whole save failed when only one part did

Title: Verify locale switch consistently applies to labels, dates and numbers without mixed language
Preconditions: System supporting multiple locales; user can switch locale
Test_Steps: 1. For each locale x surface pair, switch locale | 2. Inspect labels, date and number formats | 3. Verify no mixed-language leakage | 4. Verify RTL handling where applicable
Expected_Result: Each locale applies consistently to labels, dates and numbers across surfaces; no untranslated strings leak; RTL locales render with correct direction

Title: Verify high-risk numeric field warns on out-of-range value before save (soft stop)
Preconditions: Clinician entering vitals with configured ranges
Test_Steps: 1. Enter a vitals value just outside a plausible range | 2. Attempt to save | 3. Observe out-of-range warning | 4. Confirm or correct | 5. Repeat at the boundary and just inside
Expected_Result: Out-of-range entries trigger a soft warning prompting confirmation or correction; boundary values behave per configured thresholds; clinician can confirm intentional extremes with an explicit action

Title: Verify wrong-patient prevention prompt when active patient differs from order target
Preconditions: Two patients open in workflow; order being placed
Test_Steps: 1. For each rule set active patient and order target | 2. Place an order | 3. Observe whether a mismatch warning appears | 4. Verify final order target
Test_Data: Rules: same patient=no warn; switched-tab patient=warn; stale-context patient=warn+block
Expected_Result: When the order target does not match the displayed active patient, the system warns and requires reconfirmation, preventing wrong-patient orders; matching context proceeds normally

Title: Verify session-timeout warning gives advance notice and preserves work on extend
Preconditions: Session timeout configured; user idle on a filled form
Test_Steps: 1. Fill a form and remain idle until near timeout | 2. Observe pre-timeout warning | 3. Choose Extend session | 4. Verify form data preserved | 5. Let session expire and verify safe handling
Test_Data: Idle to near session limit; form partially filled
Expected_Result: A pre-timeout warning appears with a countdown and Extend option; extending preserves entered data; on expiry the user is safely logged out without silently losing unsaved PHI to an unauthenticated state

Title: Verify color is not the sole means of conveying status (alerts, required fields, results)
Preconditions: Pages using color-coded status (e.g. abnormal results, required fields)
Test_Steps: 1. Identify color-coded indicators (abnormal result, required field, alert severity) | 2. Verify each has a non-color cue (icon, text, pattern) | 3. Simulate color blindness | 4. Verify distinguishability
Test_Data: Indicators=abnormal flag, required asterisk, alert level
Expected_Result: Status is conveyed by text/icon/shape in addition to color; indicators remain distinguishable under color-vision deficiency simulation; meets WCAG use-of-color guidance

Title: Verify text reflows and remains usable at 200 percent zoom without loss of content
Preconditions: Browser zoom configurable; representative pages
Test_Steps: 1. Set browser zoom to 200 percent | 2. Load a clinical form and dashboard | 3. Verify content reflows without horizontal scrolling loss or clipping | 4. Verify controls remain operable
Expected_Result: At 200 percent zoom content reflows, no information or controls are lost or clipped, and the workflow remains operable, per WCAG reflow criteria

Title: Verify FHIR OperationOutcome errors are surfaced to users as readable, actionable messages
Preconditions: FHIR API integration returning OperationOutcome on error
Test_Steps: 1. Trigger a FHIR write that fails (e.g. invalid Patient resource) | 2. Capture the OperationOutcome response | 3. Verify the UI translates issue.diagnostics/severity into a user-readable message | 4. Verify no raw JSON shown to clinician
Expected_Result: FHIR OperationOutcome severity and diagnostics are mapped to a clear, actionable user message; raw resource JSON is not surfaced to clinical users; correlation is logged for support

Title: Verify failed HL7 v2 message ingestion produces an operator-friendly error queue entry
Preconditions: HL7 v2 inbound interface with an error/dead-letter queue
Test_Steps: 1. Send a malformed HL7 v2 ADT message | 2. Observe the interface engine/error queue | 3. Verify the failed message is queued with a human-readable reason | 4. Verify reprocess affordance
Test_Data: ADT^A01 with missing required MSH field
Expected_Result: The malformed message lands in an error queue with a readable failure reason and segment/field pointer; an operator can review and reprocess; no silent data loss

Title: Verify UI-confirmed void is reflected as a soft-delete (voided flag) not a hard delete in the data store
Preconditions: Backend/data access available; clinician voids a record via UI
Test_Steps: 1. Void an observation via the UI with reason | 2. Inspect the underlying record state | 3. Verify voided flag, void reason, voided_by and date_voided are set | 4. Verify row is not physically removed
Test_Data: Obs voided via UI; reason='Entered in error'
Expected_Result: The record persists with voided=true and populated void metadata rather than being physically deleted, preserving auditability and supporting later unvoid

Title: Verify deep-link/bookmark to a patient dashboard prompts login then returns to target
Preconditions: Unauthenticated user; valid deep link to a patient dashboard
Test_Steps: 1. While logged out, open a bookmarked patient dashboard URL | 2. Observe redirect to login | 3. Authenticate | 4. Verify redirect back to the originally requested patient dashboard
Test_Data: Deep link=patient dashboard URL; valid credentials
Expected_Result: Unauthenticated access redirects to login and, after successful auth, returns the user to the originally requested page with the correct patient context, avoiding re-navigation

Title: Charter-based exploratory session on alert fatigue and interruptive dialog density in clinical workflow
Preconditions: Clinician access to ordering, prescribing and documentation flows
Test_Steps: 1. Charter: explore how many interruptive warnings/confirmations a clinician encounters in a typical order-to-document flow | 2. Record each interruption, its value and dismissibility | 3. Note redundant or low-value prompts | 4. Summarize fatigue risk
Expected_Result: Findings document the count and value of interruptive prompts; low-value or redundant confirmations that risk alert fatigue and reflexive dismissal are identified for tuning while preserving high-value safety stops

Title: Verify permission-denied actions show a clear authorization message rather than failing silently
Preconditions: User lacking permission for a restricted action
Test_Steps: 1. As a user without the required privilege, attempt a restricted action (e.g. void encounter) | 2. Observe the response | 3. Verify a clear authorization message | 4. Verify no partial state change
Expected_Result: The action is blocked with a clear 'you do not have permission' message; no partial change occurs; the attempt is auditable and the control may be hidden/disabled to prevent dead-clicks
